children.heading: Antarctic Convergence
children.label: 1 Antarctic Convergence
children.text: The term “Antarctic Convergence” means a line joining the following points along the parallels of latitude and meridians of longitude: 50 degrees south, 0 degrees; 50 degrees south, 30 degrees east; 45 degrees south, 30 degrees east; 45 degrees south, 80 degrees east; 55 degrees south, 80 degrees east; 55 degrees south, 150 degrees east; 60 degrees south, 150 degrees east; 60 degrees south; 50 degrees west; 50 degrees south, 50 degrees west; and 50 degrees south, 0 degrees.1So in original. Probably should be a comma.1

children.heading: Antarctic marine living resources
children.label: 2 Antarctic marine living resources
children.text: The term “Antarctic marine living resources” means the population of finfish, molluscs, crustaceans and all other species of living organisms, including birds, found south of the Antarctic Convergence.

children.heading: Commission
children.label: 3 Commission
children.text: The term “Commission” means the Commission for the Conservation of Antarctic Marine Living Resources established pursuant to article VII of the Convention.

children.heading: Convention
children.label: 4 Convention
children.text: The term “Convention” means the Convention on the Conservation of Antarctic Marine Living Resources, done at Canberra, Australia, , and entered into force with respect to the United States on .May 7, 1980April 7, 1982

children.label: A
children.text: the harassing, molesting, harming, pursuing, hunting, shooting, wounding, killing, trapping, or capturing of Antarctic marine living resources;

children.label: B
children.text: attempting to engage in any activity set forth in subparagraph (A);

children.label: C
children.text: any other activity which can reasonably be expected to result in any activity described in subparagraph (A); and

children.label: D
children.text: any operations at sea in support of, or in preparation for, any activity described in subparagraphs (A) through (C).

children.heading: Harvest
children.label: 6 Harvest
children.text: The term “harvest” means to engage in harvesting or other associated activities.

children.heading: Import
children.label: 7 Import
children.text: The term “import” means to land on, bring into, or introduce into, or attempt to land on, bring into, or introduce into, any place subject to the jurisdiction of the United States, whether or not such landing constitutes an importation within the meaning of the customs laws of the United States.

children.heading: Person
children.label: 8 Person
children.text: The term “person” means an individual, partnership, corporation, trust, association, and any other entity subject to the jurisdiction of the United States.

children.heading: Scientific Committee
children.label: 9 Scientific Committee
children.text: The term “Scientific Committee” means the Scientific Committee for the Conservation of Antarctic Marine Living Resources established pursuant to article XIV of the Convention.

children.label: A
children.text: a vessel documented under chapter 121 of title 46 or a vessel numbered as provided in chapter 123 of that title;

children.label: i
children.text: the United States or a territory, commonwealth, or possession of the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: a State or political subdivision thereof;

children.label: iii
children.text: a citizen or national of the United States; or

children.label: iv
children.text: a corporation created under the laws of the United States or any State, the District of Columbia, or any territory, commonwealth, or possession of the United States;

children.label: C
children.text: a vessel that was once documented under the laws of the United States and, in violation of the laws of the United States, was either sold to a person not a citizen of the United States or placed under foreign registry or a foreign flag, whether or not the vessel has been granted the nationality of a foreign nation in accordance with Article 5 of the 1958 Convention on the High Seas.

children.heading: Vessel subject to the jurisdiction of the United States
children.label: 11 Vessel subject to the jurisdiction of the United States
children.text: The term “vessel subject to the jurisdiction of the United States” includes a vessel without nationality or a vessel assimilated to a vessel without nationality, in accordance with paragraph (2) of Article 6 of the 1958 Convention on the High Seas.
